  • Quick Reference: Travel Times from Hotel East 21

Japanese Stationary

Where to Find Japanese Mechanical Pencils, Traveler’s Notebooks & More

Author

DJG

Published

October 12, 2025

Your Starting Point: Hotel East 21 Tokyo

Location: Toyo, Koto-ku, Tokyo
Nearest Stations: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) - 7 min walk - Nishi-ojima Station (Toei Shinjuku Line) - 10 min walk

Your Main Transit Lines: - Tozai Line - Direct access to Otemachi (transfer hub), Nihombashi, Ginza area - Toei Shinjuku Line - Direct to Shinjuku - Both lines connect you easily to all major shopping areas

Must-Visit Shops

Traveler’s Factory (Nakameguro)

The Holy Grail for Traveler’s Notebook Fans

Location: Nakameguro, Tokyo
Nearest Station: Nakameguro Station (Hibiya Line)
Address: 〒153-0051 Tokyo, Meguro City, Kamimeguro, 3 Chome−13−10

From Hotel East 21: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Otemachi (transfer) → Hibiya Line to Nakameguro - Time: 35-40 minutes - Alternative: Toyocho → Nihombashi (transfer) → Hibiya Line to Nakameguro - Distance: About 12km

What Makes It Special: - Official flagship store of Traveler’s Company - Original Traveler’s Notebook leather covers in all sizes - Exclusive limited editions and collaborations - Every refill, accessory, and customization option - Vintage brass accessories - Factory Exclusive items you can’t get anywhere else - Watch craftspeople work on notebooks - Small cafe upstairs (coffee and atmosphere)

What to Buy: - Traveler’s Notebook leather covers (passport, regular, A5 sizes) - Refills in every style (blank, lined, grid, kraft, watercolor) - Brass clips, rulers, pens - Stickers, stamps, decorative items - Limited edition seasonal items - Customization services

Tips: - Go early or late to avoid crowds - Allow 1-2 hours to browse properly - Bring extra luggage space - Staff speak English - Very Instagrammable location

Hours: Usually 12pm-8pm (check current hours)
Website: travelers-factory.com

Itoya (Ginza)

12 Floors of Stationery Heaven

Location: Ginza, Tokyo
Nearest Station: Ginza Station (multiple lines)
Address: 2-7-15 Ginza, Chuo-ku, Tokyo

From Hotel East 21: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Direct to Nihombashi (3 stops) → Walk 10 min OR transfer to Ginza Line (1 stop to Ginza) - Time: 20-25 minutes total - Easiest route: Toyocho → Otemachi (2 stops) → Transfer to any line to Ginza (2 stops) - Distance: About 8km - This is one of your closest major shops!

What Makes It Special: - Iconic Tokyo stationery store since 1904 - 12 floors dedicated to different stationery categories - Incredible mechanical pencil selection - High-end and everyday items - Beautiful building, each floor color-coded - Main store + K.Itoya annex across the street

Floor Guide: - B1F: Greeting cards, gift wrap - 1F: Fountain pens, luxury writing instruments - 2F: Japanese paper products, washi - 3F: Mechanical pencils, ballpoint pens, pencils - 4F: Notebooks, journals, planners - 5F: Business supplies, desk accessories - 6F-11F: Various specialized items - 12F: Red Cafe with Ginza views

What to Buy: - Premium mechanical pencils (Pentel, Pilot, Uni, Zebra, Rotring) - Midori MD Notebooks - Hobonichi planners and notebooks - Washi tape and stickers - High-quality fountain pens - Unique desk accessories

Tips: - Start at top and work down, or focus on floors 3-4 for pencils/notebooks - Check out K.Itoya annex too (more lifestyle/design items) - Tax-free shopping available with passport - Can easily spend 2-3 hours here

Hours: 10am-8pm (Mon-Sat), 10am-7pm (Sun)
Website: www.ito-ya.co.jp

Tokyu Hands (Multiple Locations)

Department Store Meets DIY Haven

Best Location: Shibuya (8 floors)
Other Locations: Shinjuku, Ikebukuro, many more

From Hotel East 21 to Shibuya: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Otemachi → Transfer to Hanzomon Line → Shibuya - Time: 35-40 minutes - Alternative: Via Nihombashi with transfers - Distance: About 15km

From Hotel East 21 to Shinjuku: - Nishi-ojima Station (Toei Shinjuku Line) → Direct to Shinjuku-sanchome - Time: 25-30 minutes (this is your EASIEST route!) - Distance: About 12km - Shinjuku Tokyu Hands is most convenient for you!

What Makes It Special: - Huge department store with amazing stationery floor - Mid-range prices - Great variety without overwhelming - Easy to navigate - Multiple locations across Tokyo

What to Buy: - Good selection of mechanical pencils (all major brands) - Notebooks and planners - Masking tape and craft supplies - Unique Japanese organizational tools - DIY and craft materials - Practical everyday stationery

Tips: - Shibuya location is largest and most comprehensive - Stationery usually on 5th or 6th floor - Less overwhelming than some specialty shops - Good for “I need to pick up various items” shopping

Hours: Vary by location, typically 10am-9pm

Loft (Multiple Locations)

Trendy Lifestyle & Stationery

Best Locations: Shibuya, Ginza, Ikebukuro
Flagship: Shibuya Loft (7 floors)

From Hotel East 21: - To Ginza Loft: Same as Itoya above (20-25 minutes) - CLOSEST - To Shibuya Loft: 35-40 minutes via Tozai Line → Otemachi → Hanzomon Line - To Ikebukuro: Toyocho → Otemachi → Marunouchi Line to Ikebukuro (30 min) - Recommendation: Visit Ginza Loft - it’s your closest option!

What Makes It Special: - Curated selection of trendy stationery - Japanese and imported brands - Design-forward products - Good prices - Hip, young vibe

What to Buy: - Midori products (MD notebooks, Traveler’s items) - Cute Japanese character stationery - Innovative desk tools - Imported stationery brands - Washi tape paradise - Planners and organizers

Tips: - Stationery usually takes up 1-2 full floors - Great for gifts - Less “serious” than Itoya, more fun/colorful - Tax-free shopping available

Hours: Typically 10am-9pm

Kakimori (Kuramae)

Custom Notebooks & Artisan Ink

Location: Kuramae, Tokyo (trendy artisan neighborhood)
Nearest Station: Kuramae Station (Asakusa or Oedo Line)

From Hotel East 21: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Nihombashi → Transfer to Asakusa Line → Kuramae - Time: 25-30 minutes - Alternative: Tozai Line → Monzen-nakacho → Oedo Line → Kuramae
- Distance: About 6km - This is relatively close to your hotel!

What Makes It Special: - Custom notebook service - choose paper, binding, cover - Custom ink blending (create your own fountain pen ink color!) - Artisan, handcrafted focus - Beautiful minimal aesthetic - Small but perfectly curated

What to Buy: - Custom-made notebooks (takes 30-60 minutes) - Blended custom ink - Writing instruments - Limited selection but highest quality - Unique gifts

Tips: - Plan for time if doing custom notebook - Great for serious stationery enthusiasts - Combine with exploring Kuramae neighborhood (cafes, vintage shops) - English spoken

Hours: 11am-7pm (closed Mondays)
Website: kakimori.com

Maruzen (Multiple Locations)

Books + Premium Stationery

Best Location: Maruzen Marunouchi (near Tokyo Station)

From Hotel East 21: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Direct to Otemachi (2 stops, 5 min) → Walk 10 min to Marunouchi - Time: 15-20 minutes total - Distance: About 6km - This is your CLOSEST major shop - super convenient! - Can also get off at Tokyo Station (one more stop) and walk

What Makes It Special: - Traditional bookstore with excellent stationery section - High-quality selection - Less touristy than Itoya - Connected to Japanese literary culture - Elegant atmosphere

What to Buy: - Quality mechanical pencils - Fountain pens - Traditional Japanese paper products - Business journals and planners - Gift-quality items

Tips: - Great if you also want English books about Japan - More refined, less overwhelming - Good for serious writing enthusiasts

Sekaido (Shinjuku)

Art Supplies + Stationery

Location: Shinjuku
Nearest Station: Shinjuku Station

From Hotel East 21: - Nishi-ojima Station (Toei Shinjuku Line) → Direct to Shinjuku-sanchome (no transfers!) - Time: 25-30 minutes - Distance: About 12km - Easy direct route - no transfers needed!

What Makes It Special: - Art supplies meet stationery - 5 floors - Great prices - Less touristy - Locals’ favorite

What to Buy: - Mechanical pencils (including drafting pencils) - Sketch books and art notebooks - Technical drawing supplies - Manga supplies - Affordable quality items

Tips: - Good for artists and designers - More “working supplies” than “precious stationery” - Solid everyday prices

Specialty Items Guide

Mechanical Pencils

Where to Find Best Selection: 1. Itoya (Floor 3) - Largest variety, all price points 2. Tokyu Hands - Good mid-range selection 3. Sekaido - Technical/drafting pencils

Japanese Brands to Look For: - Pentel: GraphGear 500/1000, Orenz Nero (unbreakable lead) - Pilot: S20, S10, S5, S3 - Uni (Mitsubishi): Kuru Toga (rotating lead mechanism), Shift - Zebra: DelGuard (lead break protection) - Platinum: MSD series, Pro-Use 171 - Ohto: Conception, Promecha - Tombow: Mono Graph series

Price Ranges: - Budget: ¥300-800 ($2-6) - Mid-range: ¥800-3,000 ($6-20) - Premium: ¥3,000-10,000+ ($20-70+)

Unique Japanese Features: - Rotating lead mechanisms (Kuru Toga) - Lead break prevention systems - Ultra-precise lead grades (2H to 4B) - 0.2mm ultra-fine leads - Ergonomic grip designs

Traveler’s Notebooks

Where to Buy: 1. Traveler’s Factory - THE place, exclusive items 2. Itoya - Good selection, regular line 3. Loft - Decent selection, accessories 4. Tokyu Hands - Basic selection

What to Get: - Leather Covers: Passport, Regular (A5), or A4 sizes - Refills: Choose your paper style (grid, lined, blank, kraft, watercolor) - Brass Accessories: Clips, pen holders, rulers - Charms and Customization: Make it yours

Traveler’s Factory Exclusives: - Limited edition colors - Special collaborations - Factory-stamping service - Unique refills

Other Japanese Stationery to Explore

Notebooks: - Midori MD Notebook - Minimal design, excellent paper - Hobonichi Techo - Cult-favorite planner - Life Noble Note - Premium writing paper - Apica Premium CD - Smooth writing experience

Pens: - Pilot Juice - Smooth gel pens - Uni-ball Signo - Waterproof, fade-proof - Zebra Sarasa - Quick-dry gel ink - Pentel Energel - Fast-drying

Unique Items: - Washi Tape (mt brand is iconic) - Sticky Notes (creative shapes and designs) - Correction Tape (Japanese engineering at its finest) - Page Flags (aesthetic and functional) - Desk Organizers (minimal, beautiful)

Shopping Strategy

From Hotel East 21: Proximity Guide

Closest & Easiest (15-25 minutes): 1. Maruzen Marunouchi - 15-20 min via Tozai Line direct 2. Itoya Ginza - 20-25 min via Tozai Line → Otemachi 3. Ginza Loft - 20-25 min (same route as Itoya) 4. Kakimori Kuramae - 25-30 min via Tozai/Asakusa Lines

Medium Distance (25-35 minutes): 5. Shinjuku (Tokyu Hands, Sekaido) - 25-30 min via Toei Shinjuku Line direct (no transfers!)

Farther but Worth It (35-40 minutes): 6. Traveler’s Factory Nakameguro - 35-40 min (requires transfer) 7. Shibuya (Tokyu Hands, Loft) - 35-40 min (requires transfer)

Recommended Day Trips from Hotel East 21

Easy Half Day - Ginza Focus: - Morning: Maruzen Marunouchi (15 min) - Walk to Itoya (10 min walk) - Lunch in Ginza - Ginza Loft if time permits - All via Tozai Line - super easy!

Shinjuku Day: - Direct Toei Shinjuku Line (no transfers!) - Tokyu Hands + Sekaido + shopping/sightseeing - Easiest single-destination trip

Art & Craft Day - Kuramae: - Kakimori (25 min) - Explore artisan neighborhood - Close to hotel, easy return

Full Day - Traveler’s Notebook Pilgrimage: - Morning: Traveler’s Factory Nakameguro - Afternoon: Return via Shibuya (Tokyu Hands/Loft) - Evening: Back to hotel

Time-Based Approach

If You Have 2-3 Hours: - Go to Itoya (most comprehensive) - Or Traveler’s Factory (if Traveler’s notebooks are priority)

If You Have Half a Day: - Morning: Traveler’s Factory in Nakameguro - Afternoon: Itoya in Ginza - Stop at Loft if time permits

If You Have a Full Day: - Morning: Kakimori custom notebook experience - Lunch in Kuramae - Afternoon: Itoya for main shopping - Evening: Shibuya Loft or Tokyu Hands

Budget Planning

Budget Friendly: - Tokyu Hands, Loft, Sekaido - Expect: ¥5,000-10,000 ($35-70) for good haul

Mid-Range: - Mix of above + some premium items from Itoya - Expect: ¥15,000-30,000 ($100-200)

Splurge: - Traveler’s Factory complete setup - Premium mechanical pencils from Itoya - Custom notebooks from Kakimori - Expect: ¥30,000-50,000+ ($200-350+)

Tax-Free Shopping

Requirements: - Passport required - Minimum purchase: ¥5,000-10,000 (varies by store) - Present at time of purchase - Items must leave Japan with you

Available At: - Itoya - Loft - Tokyu Hands - Most major retailers

Practical Tips

Language

  • Major stores have English-speaking staff
  • Product names often in English/Japanese
  • Google Translate camera function helps
  • Point and smile works great!

Payment

  • Credit cards widely accepted at major stores
  • Cash still preferred at smaller shops
  • ATMs at convenience stores

Carrying Your Haul

  • Bring a foldable bag
  • Consider shipping large purchases to hotel
  • Many stores offer gift wrapping (free or small fee)

Best Times to Visit

  • Weekday mornings - least crowded
  • Avoid weekends if possible at popular spots
  • Holiday seasons (Jan, Golden Week) can be very busy

Combining with Sightseeing

Ginza Day: - Itoya → Ginza shopping → Imperial Palace nearby

Shibuya/Harajuku Day: - Loft/Tokyu Hands → Shibuya crossing → Meiji Shrine

Nakameguro Day: - Traveler’s Factory → Meguro River walk → Daikanyama cafes

Kuramae Artisan Day: - Kakimori → Vintage shops → Sumida River → Asakusa

What to Expect to Spend

Sample Shopping List:

Mechanical Pencil Enthusiast: - 3-4 mid-range pencils: ¥5,000 ($35) - Premium pencil: ¥5,000 ($35) - Lead refills variety pack: ¥1,500 ($10) - Total: ~¥11,500 ($80)

Traveler’s Notebook Starter: - Regular leather cover: ¥5,000 ($35) - 3-4 refills: ¥2,000 ($14) - Brass accessories: ¥3,000 ($20) - Customization: ¥1,000 ($7) - Total: ~¥11,000 ($76)

Comprehensive Haul: - 2-3 mechanical pencils: ¥8,000 ($55) - Traveler’s notebook setup: ¥10,000 ($70) - Midori MD notebooks: ¥3,000 ($20) - Pens and accessories: ¥5,000 ($35) - Washi tape and extras: ¥4,000 ($28) - Total: ~¥30,000 ($208)

Making the Most of Your Visit

Do Your Research

  • Check current hours (may change)
  • Look up specific items you want
  • Know your sizes (especially for Traveler’s notebooks)
  • Make a wishlist

Try Before You Buy

  • Most stores have test stations
  • Try mechanical pencils with different grips
  • Test paper quality in notebooks
  • Don’t rush

Ask for Recommendations

  • Staff are knowledgeable and helpful
  • They love enthusiasts
  • Ask about new products
  • Get insider tips

Consider Shipping

  • Large purchases can be heavy
  • Some stores ship to hotel
  • International shipping available at major stores
  • Check airline luggage limits

Quick Reference: Travel Times from Hotel East 21

Shop Time Transfers Key Line
Maruzen Marunouchi 15-20 min 0-1 Tozai Line
Itoya Ginza 20-25 min 1 Tozai → any to Ginza
Ginza Loft 20-25 min 1 Tozai → any to Ginza
Kakimori Kuramae 25-30 min 1 Tozai → Asakusa
Shinjuku (Hands/Sekaido) 25-30 min 0 Toei Shinjuku direct!
Traveler’s Factory 35-40 min 1-2 Tozai → Hibiya
Shibuya 35-40 min 1-2 Tozai → Hanzomon

Pro Tip: Your hotel’s Tozai Line connection makes Ginza area shops (Itoya, Ginza Loft, Maruzen) very convenient. For Shinjuku, use the Toei Shinjuku Line from Nishi-ojima station for a direct, no-transfer ride!

Bottom Line: Tokyo is stationery paradise. Whether you’re looking for the perfect mechanical pencil, setting up your Traveler’s Notebook system, or just exploring Japanese stationery culture, these shops will not disappoint. Budget time and luggage space accordingly—you’ll want everything!

Happy shopping! Your pencil case will thank you.
